2606.06907.title: SpectCount: Spectrotemporal Counting via Synthetic Signals Improves Large Audio Language Models
2606.06907.paper_summary: Large audio language models (LALMs) extend large language models with an audio encoder and large-scale audio data. However, the scarcity of high-quality annotated audio data remains a fundamental bottleneck for scaling. Through probing signal detectability analysis, we identify fine-grained spectrotemporal perceptual weaknesses in a foundation LALM. To address these challenges, we propose Spectrotemporal Counting (SpectCount), a data-efficient fine-tuning approach based on fully synthetic audio signals generated on-the-fly, without relying on real-world audio, annotations, or pretrained generative models. SpectCount not only resolves the observed weaknesses but also improves performance on diverse auditory benchmarks spanning sound, music, and speech, unseen during fine-tuning. These results suggest that weakness-targeted synthetic signals provide a data-efficient path toward enhanced auditory understanding capabilities in LALMs.
2606.06907.paper_summary_zh: 大型音頻語言模型（LALMs）通過音頻編碼器和大規模音頻數據擴展了大型語言模型。<lb/>然而，高質量標註音頻數據的稀缺仍然是擴展的基本瓶頸。<lb/>通過探測信號可檢測性分析，我們識別出基礎 LALM 中的細粒度頻譜時間感知弱點。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了頻譜時間計數（SpectCount），這是一種基於即時生成的全合成音頻信號的數據高效微調方法，無需依賴現實世界的音頻、標註或預訓練生成模型。<lb/>SpectCount 不僅解決了觀察到的弱點，還提高了在多樣聽覺基準（涵蓋聲音、音樂和語音）上的性能，這些基準在微調過程中未曾見過。<lb/>這些結果表明，針對弱點的合成信號為提升 LALMs 的聽覺理解能力提供了一條數據高效的途徑。
2606.06907.author: Seonuk Kim et.al.
2606.06907.authors: Seonuk Kim, Yonghyeon Jun, Ju Yeon Kang, Jimin Hong, Yoonhyeong Lee, Nam Soo Kim
